sentimentBasis: Business and investment impact for Indonesia

items.title: Telecom spectrum auction details remain key watch item
items.summary: Payment terms, reserve price, and participant appetite will shape capex pressure and network investment outlook for Indonesian telcos.
items.impact: Potential higher spectrum costs could pressure balance sheets and delay discretionary capex.

items.title: Data-center and AI infrastructure confirmations are a positive catalyst
items.summary: Official statements from PLN, Telkom/NeutraDC, BDx, or ministries would validate new AI/data-center infrastructure momentum.
items.impact: Confirmation would support power demand, connectivity, cloud infrastructure, and digital-economy investment narratives.

items.title: EV/PHEV pricing and incentive updates can shift adoption curve
items.summary: Pricing and incentive updates matter for BYD, Wuling, Hyundai, Chery/Jaecoo, Jetour, Polytron, and local suppliers.
items.impact: Sharper prices or policy support could lift adoption and local component opportunities, though margin pressure is possible.

items.title: No major verified fresh Indonesia crypto item found today
items.summary: Crypto/OJK flow is quiet today; continue monitoring OJK and official channels for verified regulatory changes.
items.impact: Low immediate market impact; absence of negative regulatory surprise is mildly stabilizing.

items.title: Infrastructure confirmations remain an investment signal to track
items.summary: Government and SOE confirmations can drive investor confidence across logistics, power, and industrial estate themes.
items.impact: Pipeline visibility matters, but impact depends on financing clarity and execution timelines.

items.title: Startup funding appetite remains selective
items.summary: Investors continue to prioritize profitability, AI enablement, and resilient revenue models in Southeast Asia.
items.impact: Selective capital can reward stronger operators while pressuring cash-burning models.
